acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md Job template updated. Spawned neo Job uses send-and-wait instead of a loop.
description: Formalise the canonical worker communication pattern in CLAUDE.md and the Job template. Workers must use bdh :aweb chat send-and-wait for mid-task Q&A (blocks at CLI, zero extra Claude invocations) and bdh :aweb mail send ordis for final handoff only. No polling loops in startup scripts. Task prompt injected by ordis must include these instructions explicitly.
title: Update worker Job template to enforce send-and-wait coordination pattern

acceptance_criteria: Ordis receives neo completion mail and autonomously spins up hawk. Hawk signals back. Ordis merges without human intervention. Woodson only sees final Discord notification.
description: Ordis must act autonomously on completion mail from neo/hawk without waiting for Woodson. On 'PR ready' mail from neo: spin up hawk Job with review task. On 'Changes requested' mail from hawk: spin up new neo Job with review feedback in prompt. On 'Approved' from hawk: merge PR and notify Woodson. Closes the full dev->review->merge loop with no human intervention required.
title: Implement ordis autonomous coordinator logic

acceptance_criteria: ADB works in agent pod without fdsan crash. Maestro can connect to emulator.
description: PR #26 (branch fix/adb-glibc-dynamic) switches agent-image Dockerfile from lzhiyong static ADB (bionic/fdsan) to glibc-linked build. Static build crashes with 'fdsan: failed to exchange ownership of file descriptor' in container. Required for Maestro ADB commands in agent pods.
title: Merge PR #26: switch ADB to glibc-linked build to fix fdsan crash

acceptance_criteria: Human<->ordis messages stay in #ordis. Agent<->agent messages go to #agent-comms threads. Ordis replies land in the correct channel. New mail from neo/hawk appears in #ordis automatically.
description:
Two related issues:
1. Messages between Woodson and ordis are being routed to #agent-comms instead of staying in #ordis. The bridge must detect if the session involves a human and route accordingly.
2. Ordis needs session context when replying — if the session is human-originated, post reply to #ordis; if agent-originated, post to the #agent-comms thread.
3. The bridge should also forward BeadHub mail addressed to ordis into #ordis so Woodson can see agent completion notifications without waiting for ordis to be triggered.
title: Fix message routing: human sessions to #ordis, agent sessions to #agent-comms, ordis replies to correct channel

acceptance_criteria: discord-bridge running without restarts. PR #27 merged.
description: PR #27 (branch fix/redis-listener-syntax) removes the orphaned worker block from redis-listener.ts that caused 13h CrashLoopBackOff. Already open on GitHub, needs merge.
title: Merge PR #27: fix discord-bridge redis-listener syntax error
